Mean.useCase: Understanding the central tendency. For accelerometer data, mean ≈ 9.8 indicates gravity (vertical orientation).
Mean.interpretation: Higher values indicate signal is mostly positive, lower values indicate mostly negative.

Median.description: Middle value when data is sorted
Median.useCase: Robust alternative to mean, less affected by outliers.
Median.interpretation: Similar to mean but ignores extreme values.

Standard deviation.description: Measure of how spread out values are
Standard deviation.useCase: High std dev = lots of movement/variation. Low std dev = stable signal.

Variance.useCase: Similar to std dev but on different scale. Measures signal energy.

Max.useCase: Detect peak accelerations or extreme events.
Max.interpretation: High max values indicate sudden movements or impacts.

Min.interpretation: Combined with max, shows signal range.

Kurtosis.useCase: Identifies outliers and extreme values. Normal distribution has kurtosis ≈ 3.

Skewness.useCase: Detect if signal is skewed toward positive or negative values.

Interquartile range.useCase: Robust measure of spread, ignores extreme outliers.
Interquartile range.interpretation: Shows where middle 50% of data lies.

Entropy.useCase: High entropy = unpredictable/random. Low entropy = regular/predictable.
Entropy.interpretation: Walking: medium entropy, Sitting: low entropy

Zero crossing rate.description: Number of times signal crosses zero
Zero crossing rate.useCase: Detects oscillatory patterns. High for periodic activities like walking.

Slope.description: Overall trend direction of the signal
Slope.useCase: Positive slope = increasing trend, negative = decreasing.
Slope.interpretation: Useful for detecting transitions (sit-to-stand has positive slope).

Mean absolute diff.useCase: Measures signal dynamics. High = rapidly changing signal.

Median absolute diff.useCase: Robust version of mean absolute diff.
Median absolute diff.interpretation: Less affected by sudden spikes in signal.

Autocorrelation.description: How similar signal is to itself at different time lags
Autocorrelation.useCase: Detects repeating patterns. Perfect for periodic activities.
Autocorrelation.interpretation: High autocorrelation = strong repeating pattern (like walking steps).

Centroid.description: Time center of mass of the signal
Centroid.useCase: Where the signal's energy is concentrated in time.
Centroid.interpretation: Indicates when most activity occurs in the window.

Positive turning points.useCase: Counts peaks in signal. Walking has many peaks.
Positive turning points.interpretation: More turning points = more complex/oscillatory pattern.

Negative turning points.interpretation: Should be similar to positive turning points for periodic signals.

Area under the curve.description: Total area under the signal curve
Area under the curve.useCase: Measures total signal magnitude over time.
Area under the curve.interpretation: Larger area = stronger signal or longer duration of activity.

Signal distance.description: Total path length of the signal
Signal distance.useCase: Measures how much the signal travels.

Root mean square.description: Square root of average of squared values
Root mean square.interpretation: Related to signal energy and amplitude.

Peak to peak distance.description: Difference between maximum and minimum values
Peak to peak distance.interpretation: Larger range = more variation in signal.

Neighbourhood peaks.description: Number of peaks in signal using neighborhood detection
Neighbourhood peaks.interpretation: Useful for counting steps in walking.

FFT mean coefficient.interpretation: Higher value = more frequency components present.

Spectral entropy.useCase: High entropy = broad frequency spectrum, low = narrow.
Spectral entropy.interpretation: Periodic activities have low spectral entropy.

Power bandwidth.description: Frequency range where most power is concentrated

Median frequency.description: Frequency that divides power spectrum in half

Spectral centroid.description: Center of mass of frequency spectrum
Spectral centroid.useCase: Where most frequency energy is located.

_default.description: Feature extracted from time series data
_default.interpretation: Refer to TSFEL documentation for detailed information.
